episode_summary: The hosts discuss iridescent golden moles and their remarkable sensory adaptations, Benjamin Franklin’s glass armonica and the myths around it, ancient Babylonian clay-envelope correspondence and other postal curiosities, and the ill-fated 1939 American Antarctic Snow Cruiser that could only travel in reverse on ice.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that golden moulds shine in all the colors of the rainbow, and they'll never know it.
My fact this week is that Mozart and Beethoven both compose music for an instrument made entirely of glass. It was invented by Benjamin Franklin.
My fact is that envelopes used to be made of clay.
My fact this week is that in 1939, America made a huge vehicle to drive across Antarctica. Unfortunately, they put no tread on the tires so it couldn't drive on ice.
